title: helianthemum oelandicum subsp. conquense
intro: Helianthemum oelandicum subsp. conquense, commonly known as the rockrose, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Helianthemum oelandicum subsp. conquense include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, and are often used to treat skin conditions, such as acne and eczema, and to soothe sore muscles and joints.
